title: ficus leichhardtii
intro: Ficus leichhardtii, also known as the Small-leaved Fig or Little-leaved Fig, is a species of fig tree native to eastern Australia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves of Ficus leichhardtii have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering health benefits including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often made into a tea or infused in hot water to take advantage of their medicinal properties, although proper identification and preparation are crucial to ensure safe and effective use.
